title: D4Vinci/Scrapling — adaptive Web Scraping framework (repo root)
notes: Project repository — authoritative for any API and feature claim. Recent commits May 11/16/18 2026 confirm active maintenance.

title: Releases · D4Vinci/Scrapling
notes: Authoritative changelog. 0.4.x added Scrapy-style spider framework, ProxyRotator, on_start/on_close/on_error hooks, async stream iteration, .to_json()/.to_jsonl() exports.

title: [Bug] Tencent WAF returns HTTP 200 with JS challenge page — Fetcher inconsistent, StealthyFetcher not truly stealthy
notes: Real production failure report. (1) Fetcher (curl_cffi) bypass is probabilistic on Tencent WAF — not deterministic. (2) StealthyFetcher uses patchright with its own .local-browsers cache, causing a second browser download even when Playwright browsers are already installed.

title: scrapling · PyPI
notes: Package metadata: Requires-Python >=3.10, extras [fetchers, ai, shell, all]. Current version 0.4.8.

title: Scrapling README — Selection methods · Fetchers · Spiders · Proxy Rotation · CLI · MCP
notes: Authoritative README. Includes performance benchmark table — see notes on S15/S16/S17 for the independence problem.

title: scrapling/__init__.py — public API exports
notes: Primary source code. Confirms top-level exports: Selector, Fetcher, AsyncFetcher, StealthyFetcher, DynamicFetcher (NOT PlayWrightFetcher — that was renamed).

title: scrapling/fetchers/__init__.py — fetcher module exports
notes: Full fetcher export list: Fetcher, AsyncFetcher, ProxyRotator, FetcherSession, DynamicFetcher, DynamicSession, AsyncDynamicSession, StealthyFetcher, StealthySession, AsyncStealthySession.

title: scrapling/core/ai.py — ScraplingMCPServer implementation
notes: Primary source for MCP server. Uses FastMCP from official MCP Python SDK. Server name 'Scrapling'. Transports: stdio (default), streamable-http on host:port. Exposes 10 tools (see report).

title: scrapling/cli.py — CLI subcommands
notes: CLI surface: install, mcp (--http/--host/--port), shell, extract {get,post,put,delete,fetch,stealth}.

title: scrapling/spiders/robotstxt.py — RobotsTxtManager
notes: Built-in robots.txt manager using the `protego` library (Scrapy's robots parser). Per-domain cache. can_fetch() honors disallow rules. Confirms robots.txt compliance is in-tree, not optional.

title: Best Python Web Scraping Library in 2026

title: Best Python Web Scraping Libraries for 2026
notes: Classifies Scrapling as 'parser layer' (outdated — ignores 0.4's fetchers + spiders + MCP). Rates parser speed as 'Med', not 'Very High'.

title: Crawlee vs. Scrapy vs. BeautifulSoup: Which Framework in 2026?

title: Scrapling: Adaptive Python web scraping library
notes: Independent review of Scrapling. Critical on the benchmark methodology: 'these numbers from Scrapling's own benchmark, so treat them as directional ... Big grain-of-salt required.' ScrapingBee is a competing managed-service vendor — bias to flag.

title: Fastest Python Web Scraping Library: Benchmarks and Recommendations
notes: Independent parser benchmark with reproducible methodology. selectolax 3-7ms < lxml 8-15ms < parsel 10-18ms < BS4+lxml 20-35ms < BS4+html.parser 45-65ms per 120KB document.

title: Top Web Parsers and API Services for Data scraping: A Comparison of Speed, Scalability, and Bypassing Protections
notes: Independent benchmark. selectolax ~0.002s vs BeautifulSoup ~0.05s on the same document — 25× spread. Used for parser-tier comparison.
